metadata.title:
COMPANY WILL ACCEPT UNION


FRIENDSHIP BUT WON'T CON-


sIDER RECOGNITION OF Or


CANNED LABOR.

pages.raw_text:
The negotiations carried on by
committees representing organized
labor and the w. w. p. Co. seem
to have had the usual result. The
company tried to hand the working
men another lemon.


At the meeting of the Central
Labor Union last night the report
Of the committee appointed to meet
officers of the w. w. p. Co. was
read 1t was stated that the COM
pany had nothing to offer that had
not been offered in the past and
that the open shop was the best
that could be expected from the
power monopoly. The company
wanted union friendship, but offered
nothing in return. Traffic Manager
McCalla said the company would
hire union men If they were needed,
but that no discrimination would pc
shown between union and nonunioR
men. The company represented it
self under a $4,000 bond to main
tain the open shop. This is the
Manufacturers association agree
ment.


The committee recommended that
the fight against the company's
street car lines be continued and
that all union men refrain from
patronizing the cars of the com
pany excepting when compelled tp
do so. No action was taken on the
report the entire matter going over
